title: centaurea chrysolepis subsp. crnogorica
intro: Centaurea chrysolepis subsp. crnogorica is a subspecies of the Centaurea chrysolepis plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is also known as the "Mountain Cornflower" and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Centaurea chrysolepis subsp. crnogorica, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting overall well-being.
